title: Queenstown June Fun
meta_desc: Discover Queenstown in June with kids: weather, events, and activities for a fun family holiday in the adventure capital of the world, with prices and tips.
intro: Queenstown is an excellent destination for a family holiday in June, with crisp winter weather and a range of activities to suit all ages. The town is less crowded than in summer, making it easier to book accommodations and popular attractions. With prices starting from $350-600 per day for a family of four, Queenstown is a great value destination for families.

sections.heading: Weather in June
sections.body:
June is winter in Queenstown, with average highs of 8°C and lows of 0°C. It's a great time to enjoy the town's winter sports and activities, such as skiing and ice skating.

The weather can be unpredictable, so it's essential to pack warm clothing, including jackets, gloves, and hats.

sections.heading: What's On in June
sections.body:
June is a great time to visit Queenstown, with a range of events and activities to suit all ages. The Queenstown Winter Festival takes place in June, featuring live music, fireworks, and ski competitions.

The Remarkables Market is also open every Saturday, offering a range of fresh food, local crafts, and kids' activities.

sections.heading: Best Activities for Families
sections.body:
Queenstown has a range of activities suitable for families with children aged 5 and above. The Skyline Gondola and Luge is a must-do, with stunning views of the town and lake.

The Queenstown Ice Arena is another great option, with ice skating and hockey sessions available.
list: Skyline Gondola and Luge: $40-$60 per person
Kjet / Dart River Jet: $100-$150 per person

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
Book your accommodations and popular attractions in advance to avoid peak season prices.

Consider purchasing a Queenstown Winter Festival pass for access to multiple events and activities.
list: Book ahead to avoid peak prices
Purchase a Queenstown Winter Festival pass
Pack warm clothing for winter weather
Take advantage of free activities, such as the Queenstown Gardens and Lake Wakatipu foreshore walk
Drive time from Queenstown Airport to town: 10-15 minutes, $20-$30 by taxi
